option_order_protocol: deterministic unique randomized order per question; canonical option index 0 is answer
methodological_note: Experiment 1b — silent audio control condition for HumMusQA attention pattern analysis. Identical to Experiment 1 in all respects EXCEPT that a single pre-existing silent WAV file is substituted for the real HumMusQA audio on every evaluation. The silence file is passed via prepare_inputs(audio_path_override=silence_path), which bypasses materialize_audio() and sets should_delete=False so the shared silence file is never removed between evaluations. Model: Qwen2_5OmniForConditionalGeneration with attn_implementation='sdpa' (eager fallback via self_attn pre-hook for decode) and disable_talker(). Attention captured via register_forward_hook on each self_attn layer (model.thinker.model.layers[i].self_attn). Hooks store output[1] (attention weights) which is non-None only at decode steps where a self_attn pre-hook injects output_attentions=True for q_len==1. With attention_mode=avllm_literal_generate (primary): model.generate() produces a short response; the first standalone A/B/C/D token's attention towards the multimodal prompt (with silent audio) is extracted from the hook storage. Text tokens are split into instruction/scaffold, question, options, and other_text using tokenizer offsets over the rendered Qwen chat template. Output schema is identical to Experiment 1 — merge_exp1_shards.py and plot_exp1_attention_pattern.py work without modification.
important_note: Experiment 1b measures answer-token attention when the audio input is silent (all zeros). Comparing to Experiment 1 (real audio) reveals whether the attention distribution is content-driven or a structural property of the architecture. If accuracy with silence ≈ 66% (same as real audio), the model responds from text alone.
